title: Olax anceps
taxonomy: Olax anceps is a species of plant in the family Olacaceae. It is a genus within the order Santalales. The family Olacaceae is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The order Santalales is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the sandalwood order. Olax anceps is one of the many species that can be found in this order and family.
uses: Olax anceps has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to be effective in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Its leaves and roots are often used to make herbal teas and infusions for their supposed health benefits. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Olax anceps as a medicinal treatment.

intro: Olax anceps, also known as the "Makalani palm," is a type of plant belonging to the family Olacaceae.
